title: Massive sell-off of semiconductor stocks: SK Hynix plummets after Nasdaq debut
summary: Chip manufacturers suffer heavy global losses led by SK Hynix's drop of over 15%, as investors reduce exposure to the sector.

title: US resumes blockade on Iran and threatens 20% toll in Strait of Hormuz, sending oil soaring
summary: Crude oil rises sharply after Trump's announcement to reinstate maritime sanctions and US airstrikes on Iranian targets.

title: Wall Street falls pressured by geopolitical tensions and fears of a rate hike
summary: Major US indices post losses in a session marked by the escalation in the Middle East and inflation expectations.

title: Capital market moves: YPF IPO, Moneybox stock market listing, and OHLA placement
summary: Several companies carry out public offerings and placements in different markets, including Argentina, the United Kingdom, Germany, and Spain.

title: Corporate lawsuits against BlackRock, Kenvue, and Deutsche Bank for alleged irregularities
summary: Large companies face litigation over cost overruns, product defects, and multimillion-dollar claims in US and German courts.

title: Movements in the energy sector: Shell sells renewables, Iraq seeks investments, and oil companies adjust budgets
summary: Energy companies restructure their operations amid the shift towards renewables and crude oil volatility.

title: Bond market: yields rise in Japan and warnings on debt and inflation
summary: Sovereign bond yields rise amid inflationary pressure, while experts recommend caution in fixed income.

title: Financial regulators in Spain, UK, and Germany announce regulatory changes
summary: Supervisory bodies reduce administrative burdens, review the legal sector, and quantify the costs of abusive practices.

title: SpaceX remains in focus of investors with bullish forecasts and debate over its valuation
summary: Two analyses highlight the long-term potential of Elon Musk's space company and its relevance in upcoming IPOs.

title: Debate on the usefulness of prediction markets
summary: Opinion pieces defend and question the role of betting markets as tools for economic and political prognosis.

title: Bitcoin falls as Germany tightens tax rules for cryptocurrencies
summary: The cryptocurrency loses value amid regulatory uncertainty and new tax reporting obligations with high fines.

title: Investment banks to see highest fee volume in four years
summary: The boom in megamergers and IPOs, including SpaceX, boosts advisory revenues on Wall Street.

title: Recommended stocks on the Spanish stock exchange: Merlin, Sacyr, IAG, and Repsol
summary: Analysts highlight the upside potential of several Ibex 35 companies and the breaking of key technical resistance levels.

title: Fed could raise rates if inflation picks up, says Governor Waller
summary: Federal Reserve Governor warns that persistent inflation would lead to further interest rate hikes.

title: European markets trade mixed: DAX steady and Ibex softens oil impact
summary: The German index closes flat while the Spanish stock market better withstands the rise in crude oil.

title: Merger of gold producers in Australia and buying interest in British companies
summary: The mining sector announces an $8.71 billion merger and global investors bid for UK companies.

title: Summer stock recommendations: two stocks with potential to double and Spanish selection
summary: Both in the US and Spain, analysts identify stocks with high seasonal revaluation potential.

title: Japan shines in sports: good performance in NBA Summer League and presence at The Open
summary: Guard Yukio Kawamura scores 10 points in the US summer league, and Hideki Matsuyama competes in The Open Championship.

title: Japan slightly reduces school waiting list, but it still affects thousands of children
summary: The number of children on nursery school waiting lists drops to 14,000, with a high concentration in Tokyo.

title: Volkswagen plans to cut 50,000 additional jobs to reduce costs
summary: The German automaker intensifies its workforce reduction plan amid cost pressure and the electric transition.
